t: A's Crush Nationals 15-1, Snap 10-Game Losing Streak
d: One night after a 23-4 humiliation, JT Ginn carried a no-hitter into the seventh and Wilson, Soderstrom and Cortes homered as the A's buried Washington 15-1.

t: Giants Blow 3-0 Lead to Mariners, Lose 4-3 in 10 Innings
d: The Giants led Seattle 3-0 behind Logan Webb, then Cole Young's three-run homer and a Julio Rodriguez walk-off sac fly buried them 4-3 in ten innings.

t: Nationals 23, A's 4: Oakland Opens the Second Half With an Embarrassing Blowout
d: Washington hung 23 runs on the Athletics in West Sacramento, Gage Jump got knocked around, and Andres Chaparro homered twice. Soderstrom and Langeliers went deep for the only bright spots in a 23-4 wipeout.

t: Willy Adames Grand Slam Powers Giants Past Mariners 7-0 to Open the Second Half
d: Willy Adames crushed his seventh career grand slam, Bryce Eldridge went deep, and Landen Roupp tossed seven shutout innings as the Giants blanked the Mariners 7-0 in Seattle to start the second half with a statement.

t: Bay Area at the 2026 MLB All-Star Game: Shea Langeliers Reached Twice, Luis Arraez Got One Swing, Logan Webb Never Threw
d: The AL beat the NL 4-0 at Citizens Bank Park on July 14, 2026. A's catcher Shea Langeliers started, walked, scored and singled. Giants second baseman Luis Arraez struck out in his only at-bat. Logan Webb never left the bullpen. A Bay Area breakdown of the 2026 All-Star Game.

t: A's at the All-Star Break: 41-55, a Nine-Game Skid, and the Mirage I Warned You About
d: The Athletics led the AL West earlier this year and stagger into the 2026 All-Star break at 41-55, fourth place, 8 games back, on a nine-game losing streak. A full first-half breakdown of the collapse and why the second half only gets uglier.

t: Giants at the All-Star Break: 41-55, a Lost First Half, and a Rookie Manager Learning on the Job
d: The Giants limp into the 2026 All-Star break at 41-55, fourth in the NL West and 20.5 games back. A full first-half breakdown, an honest look at Tony Vitello's rookie season, and what the second half has to prove before the August 3 deadline.

t: Raiders 2026 Season Preview: Klint Kubiak, Kirk Cousins, and a Rookie Named Mendoza Try to Fix a 3-14 Mess
d: A Bay Area take on the 2026 Las Vegas Raiders: new coach Klint Kubiak, Kirk Cousins holding the fort for No. 1 pick Fernando Mendoza, Ashton Jeanty in the backfield, and a franchise that left Oakland trying to climb out of a 3-14 hole in a loaded AFC West.

t: The A's Lost 1-0 Again, Eight Straight Now, and They Are Coming Back to Earth Fast
d: The Athletics got four-hit by five White Sox pitchers in a 1-0 loss on July 11, their eighth straight defeat. Gage Jump deserved better, and the team that beat the Dodgers on July 1 has crashed back to earth.

t: I Told You the A's Were a Mirage: Nine Straight Losses, 9-1 to the White Sox, and No Bottom in Sight
d: The Athletics have lost nine in a row after a 9-1 beatdown by the White Sox on July 12, and if you have been paying attention this was never a surprise. This was always coming. A column on the A's tailspin, the free fall to 41-53, and why it keeps getting worse.

t: The Giants Bullpen Finally Held One: Erik Miller Slams the Door in a 3-1 Series Win Over the Rockies
d: After a sickening ninth-inning collapse to open the series, the Giants answered with back-to-back wins, capped by a 3-1 win over the Rockies on July 12. Trevor McDonald went seven, Willy Adames had three hits, and Erik Miller closed it out without the bullpen blowing it for once.

t: Tyler Mahle Finally Won a Game, and Casey Schmitt Made Sure It Counted
d: Tyler Mahle threw seven strong innings for his first win since April 22, Casey Schmitt broke a 1-1 tie with a three-run homer, and the Giants beat the Rockies 4-2 at Oracle Park on July 11.

t: Casey Schmitt Is Having an All-Star Breakout Season, Even If the All-Star Game Didn't Notice
d: Casey Schmitt's 2026 breakout season for the Giants: 19 home runs, a .494 slugging percentage, a 118 wRC+, and an All-Star case the NL roster ignored. The best story on a bad San Francisco team.

t: Jerry Rice Says This Is the Year the 49ers Break the Super Bowl Drought, and He Would Know
d: Jerry Rice told NBC Sports Bay Area at Lake Tahoe that 2026 'is the year for the Niners' to win it all. The greatest 49er ever is calling the end of a Super Bowl drought that stretches back to January 1995.

t: This Might Be the Best Team Kyle Shanahan Has Ever Had
d: Purdy, a healthy McCaffrey, Mike Evans and Christian Kirk, and Bosa and Warner back from injury. On paper this is the deepest roster of the Shanahan era. If the 49ers finally stay healthy, this could be the year they win it all.

t: A's at White Sox, July 10: Six Straight Losses and Jacob Lopez's 7.04 ERA Walk Into Chicago
d: The Athletics carry a six-game losing streak and a 41-52 record into Guaranteed Rate Field, handing the ball to Jacob Lopez and his 7.04 ERA against Sean Burke and the White Sox. A preview of Friday night's mess in Chicago.

t: The Giants Blew Another One, and It Is the Same Sickening Story
d: Caleb Kilian coughed up a ninth-inning lead against the worst team in baseball, the Giants lost 4-3 to the Rockies, and it is time to say the quiet part out loud about Tony Vitello and Buster Posey's leverage-free bullpen.

t: Rafael Devers Looks Wide Awake Again for the Giants
d: Rafael Devers drove another one out at Oracle Park against the Rockies, his 19th of the season, and after a short quiet stretch the Giants slugger looks locked in again. This is the hitter San Francisco paid for.

t: The A's Got Swept and Have Now Lost Six Straight, 4-1 to the Tigers
d: Framber Valdez retired the first 11 A's, Jake Rogers and Zach McKinstry went deep, and a pinch-hitter homered in his first big-league at-bat. Detroit swept the Athletics 4-1. Six losses in a row and no end in sight.

t: The Giants Might Actually Have Something in Carson Whisenhunt
d: Rookie lefty Carson Whisenhunt gave the Giants 5 2/3 sharp innings, Casey Schmitt, Bryce Eldridge and Willy Adames went deep, and San Francisco beat the Rockies 8-2. For once, there was a reason to watch.

t: A Rookie Named Melton Made the A's Look Like a Beer-League Team, 6-1
d: Troy Melton struck out nine, Jeffrey Springs got shelled for six runs, and the Athletics lost 6-1 in Detroit. A century of Oakland baseball, and this is what is left.

t: Dylan Cease Nearly No-Hit One of the Worst Giants Teams I Can Remember
d: Toronto's Dylan Cease carried a no-hitter into the ninth and the Blue Jays hammered Logan Webb and the Giants 10-0. This is a bad, boring, going-nowhere baseball team, and Wednesday proved it.

t: Team of the Decade: When the 49ers Ran the NFL
d: Five Super Bowls in fourteen seasons. Joe Montana, Steve Young, and Jerry Rice turned the San Francisco 49ers into the gold standard of a generation.

t: The 49ers Are Still Paying For Vegas
d: The 49ers had the Chiefs on the ropes in Las Vegas and let it go. Two years later that overtime in Allegiant Stadium still shapes everything this team does.

t: The A's Play in Sacramento Now, and That's the Whole Ugly Story
d: A century of Oakland baseball, packed into a minor-league park in West Sacramento while everyone waits for Las Vegas. This is the Bay Area sports betrayal nobody voted for.

t: Barry Bonds: The Loudest Bat San Francisco Ever Saw
d: 762 home runs, 73 in a single season, seven MVPs. Barry Bonds gave the Giants the most feared bat in baseball history. A look back at his years in San Francisco.

t: Why the Bay Area Is One of the Greatest Sports Regions in America
d: 49ers dynasties, three Giants World Series titles, and a Warriors era that reshaped the NBA. A look at the rich championship history of Bay Area sports.

t: Brandon Aiyuk Has Gone Full Antonio Brown, and It's Hard to Watch
d: Brandon Aiyuk once looked like one of the 49ers' cleanest success stories. Since the Super Bowl loss to Kansas City, the story has turned into something much stranger.

t: Bochy's October Wizardry and the Core Four Bullpen That Won Three Titles
d: Bruce Bochy managed the Giants bullpen like a chess grandmaster. How Javier Lopez, Santiago Casilla, Sergio Romo, and Jeremy Affeldt became the Core Four behind three World Series titles.

t: Flashback: Madison Bumgarner Owned the 2014 World Series
d: A 0.43 ERA, a complete-game shutout, and five scoreless innings out of the bullpen on two days' rest to save Game 7. Madison Bumgarner's 2014 World Series is the greatest October a Giant ever had.

t: Flashback: The Night Klay Thompson Scored 37 in a Single Quarter
d: January 23, 2015. Klay Thompson went 9-for-9 from three and dropped 37 points in one quarter against the Kings, the most in a quarter in NBA history. Twelve minutes of pure basketball insanity.

t: Flashback: The Catch, and the Dynasty It Started
d: January 10, 1982, Candlestick Park. Joe Montana to Dwight Clark, 28-27 over the Cowboys, and the birth of the 49ers dynasty. A look back at the most important play in Bay Area football history.

t: The 1993 Pennant Race and Dusty Baker's Salomon Torres Gamble on the Final Day
d: The 1993 Giants won 103 games and still missed October. Inside the last great pennant race, Dusty Baker's decision to start rookie Salomon Torres on the final day, and the 12-1 loss to the Dodgers that ended it all.

t: Even-Year Magic: The Giants Dynasty of the Early 2010s
d: Three World Series titles in five years. The San Francisco Giants of 2010, 2012, and 2014 turned odd, lovable rosters into a modern dynasty.

t: The Giants Keep Promising October. Oracle Park Is Still Waiting.
d: San Francisco keeps assembling teams that are fine. Fine does not fill Oracle Park in September, and Giants fans have earned the right to want more.

t: The Giants Woke Up the Worst Offense in Baseball, and It Cost Them
d: The Blue Jays had scored two runs in 27 innings and looked broken at the plate. Then Trevor McDonald and the Giants let them hang nine in a 9-3 loss at Oracle Park.

t: Giants vs Rockies, July 10: Robbie Ray Has to Bury Colorado, and Bryce Eldridge Is the Reason to Watch
d: The Giants host the last-place Rockies at Oracle Park on July 10 with Robbie Ray on the mound. This is a game they have to win, and rookie Bryce Eldridge, all 6-foot-7 of him, is turning inside pitches into splash hits.

t: This Giants Season Is Over. Build Around Bryce Eldridge and Explain the Bullpen.
d: At 35-49 and 17 games back, the Giants season is done. The path forward runs through Bryce Eldridge, and somebody needs to explain how Buster Posey thought a bullpen with no leverage arms would survive a full season.

t: Jeff Kent: The MVP Nobody Had to Like
d: Jeff Kent won the 2000 NL MVP in San Francisco, hit more home runs than any second baseman in history, and feuded with Barry Bonds. A look back at a prickly Giants great.

t: LeBron and Curry on the Same Team: What It Would Mean for Their Legacies and the NBA
d: LeBron James is leaving the Lakers and the Warriors want him next to Stephen Curry. If it happens, it reshapes two legacies and the balance of the entire NBA. Here is what pairing them would really mean.

t: Two Hall of Famers, One Job: The Montana-Young Controversy and the Trade That Split the Bay
d: The 49ers had two Hall of Fame quarterbacks on one roster in Joe Montana and Steve Young, and then they traded Montana to Kansas City. Inside the most agonizing quarterback controversy in NFL history.

t: The NFL Blackballed Colin Kaepernick for Kneeling, and Everyone Knows It
d: Colin Kaepernick was a 29-year-old Super Bowl quarterback with a 16-to-4 touchdown-to-interception season, and the NFL never signed him again after he kneeled for the anthem. That is not talent. That is a blackball.
k: 49ers · What Everyone Is Afraid To Say

t: The Sharks Rebuild Finally Has a Pulse, and His Name Is Macklin Celebrini
d: San Jose missed the playoffs for a seventh straight year, but a 19-year-old just broke Joe Thornton's franchise scoring record. The tank is over. The hard part starts now.

t: I Didn't Like the Tony Vitello Hire, and I'm Not Going to Pretend Otherwise
d: The Giants handed the manager job to a college coach with zero days of pro experience. Bold is not the same as smart, and this one felt like a gimmick from the jump.

t: Tony Vitello Was Not Ready to Manage in the Majors, and He Told Us So Himself
d: Tony Vitello said he can't talk down to his Giants players the way he did in college. That one honest admission tells you everything about why he was not ready to manage at the big league level.

t: 73-9: The Best Regular Season Ever, and Then the Warriors Added Kevin Durant
d: The 2015-16 Warriors went 73-9 and broke the Bulls' record for the greatest regular season in NBA history. Then they lost the Finals, signed Kevin Durant, and became unstoppable.

t: From Rick Barry to the Splash Brothers: Warriors Championship History
d: A 1975 upset for the ages and a modern dynasty led by Stephen Curry. Inside the championship history of the Golden State Warriors.

t: The Warriors Are Out of Easy Answers, and That's the Whole Story
d: The dynasty bought Golden State years of benefit of the doubt. That grace period is over, and what comes next is the most interesting question in the Bay.

t: Welcome to Bay Area Sports Blog
d: Bay Area Sports Blog covers the 49ers, Warriors, Giants, A's, Sharks, Stanford, Cal, betting angles, fan reactions, and the biggest stories across Bay Area sports.
